title: Geometric Security Model: Isostatic Rigidity in Cognitive Systems
abstract: Structural integrity principles applied to cognitive prosthetics

body.text: This paper applies geometric and structural engineering principles to cognitive prosthetics, establishing that isostatic rigidity provides the optimal balance between stability and adaptability for cognitive systems.

Structural engineering teaches us that isostatic structures - those with exactly enough constraints to be stable but not over-constrained - provide the ideal balance between strength and flexibility. This principle applies directly to cognitive prosthetic design.
Isostatic rigidity: constraint count equals degree of freedom count
Tetrahedral (K₄) mesh as isostatic structure in 3D space
Delta mesh topology as extension of isostatic principles

P31 Labs artifacts implement geometric security through balanced constraint systems that provide cognitive stability without over-restriction, allowing for learning and adaptation while maintaining functional integrity.
